title: Kinostemon ningpoensis
intro: Kinostemon ningpoensis, also known as the Ningpo magnolia, is a deciduous tree native to China, belonging to the family Magnoliaceae. This plant has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce fever, relieve cough, and alleviate respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Kinostemon ningpoensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Kinostemon ningpoensis is often prepared as a decoction or made into a tea, or used as an ingredient in various herbal formulas, such as those used to treat coughs and respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Kinostemon ningpoensis is a species of plant in the family Annonaceae. It is native to China and is classified as a flowering plant within the order Magnoliales. The plant is also known as Ningpo custard apple, and it belongs to the genus Kinostemon. Further classification of Kinostemon ningpoensis includes it being a member of the family Annonaceae and the order Magnoliales. The classification is based on the plant's morphology and characteristics.
